intro: Bubbia pancheri, a plant native to Madagascar, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Bubbia pancheri include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
